title: Right-size ECS Fargate tasks and protect placement
description: Match task cpu/memory to the supported Fargate combinations, reserve subnet IP headroom, and use health checks plus minimumHealthyPercent for safe rollouts.
body: Use when defining or scaling an ECS service on Fargate. Do not pick arbitrary cpu/memory pairs (Fargate only allows specific combinations) and avoid deploying into a /28 subnet that starves task ENIs. Actions: choose a valid cpu/memory pair, set container healthCheck and an ALB target-group health check, configure deploymentConfiguration minimumHealthyPercent/maximumPercent for rolling safety, and ensure the task subnets have free IPs and a route to ECR (VPC endpoint or NAT). Validation signals include tasks reaching RUNNING and passing target-group health, no PENDING tasks from capacity or IP exhaustion, and a clean rolling deploy. Risk boundary: scaling a production service or editing its task role is human-gated.
